sourceMovie.title: Mean Girls
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Tina Fey’s endlessly quotable high-school satire—sharp social observation disguised as pink plastic, with a cast that became instant pop culture.

recommendations.title: Easy A
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Whip-smart teen comedy with literary flair and Emma Stone owning every frame. If you liked Mean Girls’ verbal velocity, this is a perfect double feature.

recommendations.title: Clueless
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The queen bee template, Austenified—kind-hearted satire with iconic outfits and one-liners that still circulate decades later.

recommendations.title: The DUFF
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Labels, high-school hierarchies, and a rom-com heart—lighter than Mean Girls but built from the same social petri dish.

recommendations.title: Booksmart
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Female friendship front and center, chaotic energy, and jokes that land with generosity instead of cruelty—Gen-Z’s answer to the teen classic.

recommendations.title: 10 Things I Hate About You
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Shakespeare in a Seattle high school—romantic, funny, and packed with charisma. Great if you want teen comedy with more swoon.

recommendations.title: Legally Blonde
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Pink-as-armor feminism and underestimated heroine energy—broad comedy with a surprisingly sturdy spine, like Mean Girls with law school.

recommendations.title: Election
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Dark satire beneath a student council race—Payne’s edge is meaner than Mean Girls, but the social anthropology rhymes.

recommendations.title: Bridesmaids
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Adult friendships and set-piece comedy chaos—if you liked ensemble chemistry and cringe-laughs, Wiig and McCarthy deliver.

recommendations.title: Do Revenge
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Neon Y2K revival energy with revenge plotting and teen-movie self-awareness—feels like Mean Girls’ chaotic younger cousin.

recommendations.title: White Chicks
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Broad, absurd, and committed—less satirical scalpel, more slapstick. If you want the same era’s comedy turned up to cartoon volume.

faq.question: What movies capture Mean Girls’ vibe?
faq.answer: Easy A and Clueless are the closest matches for witty teen satire; Booksmart updates the friendship comedy for a new generation.

faq.question: Is there a Mean Girls musical movie?
faq.answer: A musical adaptation exists—if you want the stage energy on screen, pair it with the original to compare how the jokes land in each format.

faq.question: What should I watch for more Tina Fey writing?
faq.answer: 30 Rock is essential; Baby Mama and Date Night showcase her comic voice in different genres.
